title: OSMM Marketing Strategy Object
description: A time-boxed set of strategic marketing choices for a planning horizon: the business and marketing objectives pursued, how the brand positions and differentiates, and which audiences and growth bets it prioritizes. Canonical schema for object_type 'marketing_strategy'. The osmm-marketing-strategy-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

marketing_strategy_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'MKS-' + lowercase hyphen slug, scoped to owning business plus horizon. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: A readable label for the strategy, usually entity + horizon (e.g. 'IBM 2026 Marketing Strategy').

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context Object. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists.

linked_brand_context.description: Optional. Id of the Brand Context Object this strategy applies. Use 'BRC-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' if expected but unbuilt.

time_horizon.description: The planning period this strategy covers — a year, half, quarter, or named initiative window (e.g. 'FY2026', 'FY2026 H1', 'Always-on 2026').

business_objectives.description: The business goals marketing is in service of. Outcomes the business cares about, drawn from (not a copy of) Business Context strategic priorities.

marketing_objectives.description: Marketing's own prioritized goals for the horizon. Outcomes, not activities.

success_criteria.description: Optional. Strategy-level description of what success looks like, kept qualitative/directional. Metric definitions and numeric targets live in the Measurement Framework.

positioning_statement.description: One to three sentences: how the brand chooses to compete this horizon. Distinct from the durable brand_promise and standing value_proposition.

differentiation_strategy.description: Optional. The concrete ways the brand will win vs. its competitive set, expressed as what marketing will lead with.

priority_audiences.description: Optional. Ids of the prioritized Audience Objects. Use 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' for an unbuilt audience.

growth_priorities.description: Optional. The growth bets — dayparts, segments, products, or motions the brand will lean into.

linked_measurement_framework.description: Optional. Id of the Measurement Framework Object that operationalizes these objectives into KPIs. Use 'MEF-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until that object is built.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
